mappings.rationaleAI: The human body's organ systems as the sites of diseases affecting any organ or tissue (E) and diseases of particular bodily systems (F).
Arthritis as a heterogeneous group of joint disorders within diseases of the supportive-protective system joints (F.8.c).
Cancer as uncontrolled cell proliferation and tumours affecting any organ or tissue of the body (E).
Cardiovascular disease as the leading global cause of death, relevant to diseases of the cardiovascular system (F.1).
Allergy as a hypersensitivity disorder producing allergenic diseases and anaphylactic shock (D.11).
Digestive processes relevant to diseases of the digestive system from mouth through large intestine and digestive glands (F.4).
Characteristics, causes, and classifications of human disease (A), detection and diagnosis (B), and symptoms of diseases of the body as a whole (D).
Pneumonia as an infectious disease of the respiratory system caused by bacteria, viruses, and fungi (F.3.a).
Physical disabilities affecting respiratory, neurological, and sensory function relevant to disorders of the nervous system (F.9).
CVD encompassing diseases of the heart and great vessels (F.1.a) and blood vessels (F.1.b) as the leading global cause of death.
Diabetes as a metabolic disease (D.2) involving the pancreas within diseases of the endocrine system (F.5.f).
Injuries caused by physical agents including electrical shock, temperature extremes, radiation, and wounds (D.8).
Bone pathologies including fractures, osteoporosis, and cancer relevant to diseases of the skeletal system and bone (F.8.a).
Pregnancy complications including gestational diabetes, hypertension, and anemia as diseases of pregnancy (F.6.e).
Hormonal dysregulation underlying diseases of the endocrine system from pituitary through pancreas (F.5).
Tuberculosis as a communicable infectious disease (D.5) and an infectious disease of the respiratory system (F.3.a).
Chronic alcohol consumption causing organ damage relevant to poisoning and toxic effects (D.10).
Myocardial infarction as ischemic death of cardiac muscle, a disease of the heart and great vessels (F.1.a).
Gastroenteritis as an acute infectious contagious disease (D.5) affecting the digestive system (F.4).
STIs as communicable contagious diseases (D.5) transmitted through sexual contact, relevant to infections and sexually transmitted diseases of the reproductive system (F.6.b).
Dentistry treating diseases of the mouth, pharynx, and associated structures within the digestive system (F.4.a).
Nutritional diseases including disorders related to nutritional deficiencies and excesses (D.3).
Smoking causing bronchopulmonary diseases including COPD and lung cancer (F.3.c).
Viruses as agents of communicable infectious diseases including HIV, influenza, and hepatitis (D.5).
Lung diseases including pneumonia, COPD, cancer, and emphysema as diseases of the respiratory system (F.3).
Disorders of the nervous system including neuropathy, demyelination, and neurodegeneration (F.9).
Muscle disorders including atrophy and hypertrophy as diseases of muscle within the supportive-protective system (F.8.f).
Medical imaging modalities including X-ray, MRI, CT, and ultrasound as tools for detection and diagnosis of disease (B).
The common cold as the most frequent communicable infectious disease (D.5) affecting the upper respiratory tract (F.3.a).
Health as the baseline from which disease characteristics, causes, and classifications are defined (A).
Mental health disorders relevant to disorders of the central nervous system (F.9.d).
Obesity as a metabolic disease (D.2) with nutritional excess dimensions (D.3).
Pharmaceutical drugs for pharmacodynamic therapy (C.3) and chemotherapy (C.4) in the treatment of disease.
Bacteria as agents of communicable infectious diseases including pathogenic strains (D.5).
Disorders of the ear and hearing including hearing loss, tinnitus, and vertigo (F.9.g).
Skin disorders as diseases of the integument within the supportive-protective system (F.8.g).
Wheat gluten triggering coeliac disease and related autoimmune conditions as nutritional diseases (D.3).
Unsafe drinking water as a primary cause of diarrheal communicable disease (D.5).
Lactose intolerance and dairy allergy as nutritional disorders related to food composition (D.3).
Influenza as a communicable respiratory infectious disease (D.5) causing seasonal epidemics and respiratory system infection (F.3.a).
HIV as a retrovirus causing communicable infectious disease (D.5) and a sexually transmitted infection (F.6.b).
Smallpox as a communicable viral disease eradicated through vaccination, exemplifying contagious disease control (D.5).
Vaccines as biological therapy conferring active acquired immunity for disease treatment and prevention (C.2).
